text: Catalysts speed up reactions without being used up. How do they do it? In both industry and biology, they’re essential. Think of them as secret agents in chemical reactions. Without catalysts, many reactions would take ages. They lower the energy needed to start, speeding up processes. For example, enzymes are biological catalysts vital for digestion. Without them, we couldn’t break down food. Catalysts are everywhere! They’re not just in labs; they shape our world by making reactions efficient. Curious to learn more about chemistry? Hit that follow button for more fascinating science!
